new_articles.title: A home that washes, cools and thinks for you: Inside LG's 'Zero Labor Home' vision for India
new_articles.summary: LG Electronics 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'A home that washes, cools and thinks for you: Inside LG's 'Zero Labor Home' vision for India'이다. LG Electronics is building a future home powered by artificial intelligence. Intelligent appliances, connected platforms, and robots will work together to automate chores. This "Zero Labor Home" vision aims for ultimate convenience. India is a key market for these advanced AI-driven living solutions. LG's manufacturing facilities are aligning with this global AI roadmap. Reuters LG Electronics logo Seoul, South Korea: LG Electronics unveiled its vision for an AI-powered "Zero Labor Home", where intelligent appliances, connected platforms and autonomous robots work together to anticipate household needs and automate everyday chores, underscoring the company's ambition to move beyond appliances and become a provider of AI-driven living solutions The Korean electronics giant said the future home will be built around an evolving AI ecosystem that goes beyond connecting devices to understanding user behaviour, learning household routines and proactively taking action to simplify daily life "India is entering a transformative phase of home appliance premiumisation, catalysed by rising household incomes, rapid urbanisation and a clear consumer desire to modernise living spaces," said Young Min Hwang, Director, Home Solutions, LG Electronics India According to LG Electronics CEO Lyu Jae-cheol, the company aims to double combined revenue from the three markets by 2030 as it deepens its focus on high-growth economies across the Global South "Our combined revenue from these regions reached KRW 6.

new_articles.title: LG Electronics aims to double combined revenue in India, Brazil, Saudi Arabia by 2030
new_articles.summary: LG Electronics 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'LG Electronics aims to double combined revenue in India, Brazil, Saudi Arabia by 2030'이다. For LG, hyper-growth in major emerging economies like India is a strategic move to create a balanced, resilient regional portfolio, says Global CEO Lyu Jae-cheol. ece Lyu Jae-cheol, Global CEO, LG Electronics Consumer durables major LG Electronics is aiming to double the combined revenues in key growth markets of India, Brazil and Saudi Arabia by 2030 This comes at a time when the South Korean major is looking at strengthening global earnings resilience amidst volatile macroeconomic environment through strategic measures including cost competitiveness and future growth engines such as AI Home ecosystem The company’s Indian subsidiary was listed last year LG Electronics IR Communications Team Leader Aeron Kim said the company will be doubling its production capacity in India to serve not just domestic demand but also grow share of exports significantly from the current levels of 6-7 per cent Besides shipping premium products, such as side-by side refrigerators and large capacity washing machines, to developed markets, it is also exporting India designed “Essential Series” to emerging markets. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: Why India is the centerpiece of LG's multi billion-dollar vision for the Global South
new_articles.summary: LG Electronics 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Why India is the centerpiece of LG's multi billion-dollar vision for the Global South'이다. LG India growth strategy and consumer electronics market expansion: LG Electronics is targeting a significant revenue increase in India, Saudi Arabia, and Brazil by 2030. India is a crucial market, offering opportunities for both new buyers and upgrades. The company is adapting its products and manufacturing to meet local needs. This strategy aims for profitable and sophisticated growth in emerging economies. A senior LG Electronics executive told ET Online that the South Asian nation was their top market in the world in terms of home air-conditioners As new apartment blocks rise across Gurugram, Hyderabad's fast-growing suburbs and the outskirts of Lucknow, they are also revealing the contours of LG Electronics' growth strategy—one increasingly tied to India's expanding middle class and housing market "For LG, this hyper-growth in major emerging economies like India is not just about expanding our footprint; it is a strategic pivot to build a highly balanced, resilient regional portfolio that complements our established strongholds in Korea, North America, and Europe," said Lyu Jae-cheol, CEO at LG Electronics "India, alongside Saudi Arabia and Brazil, is at the absolute forefront of LG Electronics' long-term growth strategy LG Electronics India 's net profit declined 8% year-on-year in the March quarter as rupee weakness and elevated commodity prices squeezed margins. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.summary: Coupang 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 '‘South Korea’s Amazon’ hit with record fine over data breach'이다. Coupang told to pay $409mn after hack that exposed personal information of nearly two-thirds of population. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다. 가능한 경우 본문에서 회사 언급 전후 문맥을 함께 반영했다.

new_articles.title: South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country's largest data breach penalty
new_articles.summary: Coupang 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country's largest data breach penalty'이다. The Personal Information Protection Commission said the New York-listed company had leaked personal data of more than 33 million customers and failed to detect the breach within the 72 hours required by the law. "This accident occurred due to Coupang's lack of safety measures and systems, not ​sophisticated hacking," Song Kyung-hee, the chairperson of the privacy regulator, told a briefing on Thursday After the fine was announced, ​Coupang apologised for having caused concern to the public and its customers Song said Coupang's security system allowed a hacker to easily access the personal information of all of its customers, even after the suspect left the company South Korea said, however, its Coupang probe was neither a trade nor security issue ​and should be handled separately from the ongoing talks with Washington "Coupang has grown its e-commerce service significantly based on vast customer data," Song said. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: South Korea hits e-commerce giant Coupang with record US$409 million fine for data breach
new_articles.summary: Coupang 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'South Korea hits e-commerce giant Coupang with record US$409 million fine for data breach'이다. South Korea imposed on Thursday a record US$409 million fine on e-commerce giant Coupang over a leak that exposed the data of more than 30 million customers, provoking ire from US lawmakers. The move caps a months-long probe into the country’s largest online retail platform, which is incorporated in the United States. Allegations of a massive data leak first surfaced in November, becoming an unexpected source of friction between Seoul and Washington. “We have decided to impose a total of 624. 68. .

new_articles.title: South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country's largest data breach penalty
new_articles.summary: Coupang 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country's largest data breach penalty'이다. Channel NewsAsia is an English-language news organization based in Singapore. SEOUL: South Korea hit e-commerce giant Coupang with a record US$408 million fine on Thursday (Jun 11) over a leak that allegedly exposed more than 30 million customers' data and has provoked the ire of US lawmakers. Coupang, the country's largest online retail platform, signalled in a statement that it would. But Coupang "delayed breach notifications", she said "After receiving the commission's official ruling, we expect the facts to be clearly established through legal procedures," Coupang added Related: Coupang says all leaked customer information has been deleted US DISPUTE The commission also said Coupang had "unlawfully collected the online activity records of about 11 Washington is said to have warned it would halt such talks unless the legal protections of Coupang chairman Kim Bom - an American citizen also known as Kim Bom-suk - were guaranteed The Coupang fine will "again draw backlash from the US" for its size, said Kim Dae-jong, professor of business at Sejong University in Seoul. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: S. Korea fines e-commerce giant Coupang record $525 million over data leak
new_articles.summary: Coupang 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'S. Korea fines e-commerce giant Coupang record $525 million over data leak'이다. The move caps a months-long probe into the country’s largest online retail platform. PHOTO: AFP Published Jun 11, 2026, 10:48 AM Updated Jun 11, 2026, 05:43 PM SEOUL – South Korea imposed on June 11 a record US$408 million (S$525 million) fine on e-commerce giant Coupang over a leak that exposed the data of more than 30 million customers, provoking ire from US lawmakers on Coupang for violating safety obligations and collecting personal data without legal grounds,” a Personal Information Protection Commission statement said 5 million users being exposed, the commission said Its probe also found that “Coupang had unlawfully collected the online activity records of about 11 The US is said to have warned it could halt such discussions unless the legal protections of Coupang chairman Kim Bom – an American citizen also known as Kim Bom-suk – were guaranteed. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: SK Hynix to triple wafer capacity by 2034: Chairman Chey
new_articles.summary: SK hynix 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'SK Hynix to triple wafer capacity by 2034: Chairman Chey'이다. TOKYO -- South Korea's SK Hynix will triple its wafer capacity by 2034 to meet soaring demand for its memory chips critical for artificial intelligenc. Japan 'excellent candidate' for South Korean chipmaker's overseas plants. (Photo by Yuki Nakao) KIM JAEWON June 11, 2026 05:01 JST TOKYO -- South Korea's SK Hynix will triple its wafer capacity by 2034 to meet soaring demand for its memory chips critical for artificial intelligence computing, SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won said in an exclusive interview with Nikkei Asia on Wednesday. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

This strategy aims for profitable and sophisticated growth in emerging economies. A senior LG Electronics executive told ET Online that the South Asian nation was their top market in the world in terms of home air-conditioners As new apartment blocks rise across Gurugram, Hyderabad's fast-growing suburbs and the outskirts of Lucknow, they are also revealing the contours of LG Electronics' growth strategy—one increasingly tied to India's expanding middle class and housing market "For LG, this hyper-growth in major emerging economies like India is not just about expanding our footprint; it is a strategic pivot to build a highly balanced, resilient regional portfolio that complements our established strongholds in Korea, North America, and Europe," said Lyu Jae-cheol, CEO at LG Electronics "India, alongside Saudi Arabia and Brazil, is at the absolute forefront of LG Electronics' long-term growth strategy LG Electronics India 's net profit declined 8% year-on-year in the March quarter as rupee weakness and elevated commodity prices squeezed margins. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

matched_articles.title: South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country's largest data breach penalty
matched_articles.summary: Coupang 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country's largest data breach penalty'이다. The Personal Information Protection Commission said the New York-listed company had leaked personal data of more than 33 million customers and failed to detect the breach within the 72 hours required by the law. "This accident occurred due to Coupang's lack of safety measures and systems, not ​sophisticated hacking," Song Kyung-hee, the chairperson of the privacy regulator, told a briefing on Thursday After the fine was announced, ​Coupang apologised for having caused concern to the public and its customers Song said Coupang's security system allowed a hacker to easily access the personal information of all of its customers, even after the suspect left the company South Korea said, however, its Coupang probe was neither a trade nor security issue ​and should be handled separately from the ongoing talks with Washington "Coupang has grown its e-commerce service significantly based on vast customer data," Song said. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

matched_articles.summary: Coupang 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country's largest data breach penalty'이다. Channel NewsAsia is an English-language news organization based in Singapore. SEOUL: South Korea hit e-commerce giant Coupang with a record US$408 million fine on Thursday (Jun 11) over a leak that allegedly exposed more than 30 million customers' data and has provoked the ire of US lawmakers. Coupang, the country's largest online retail platform, signalled in a statement that it would. But Coupang "delayed breach notifications", she said "After receiving the commission's official ruling, we expect the facts to be clearly established through legal procedures," Coupang added Related: Coupang says all leaked customer information has been deleted US DISPUTE The commission also said Coupang had "unlawfully collected the online activity records of about 11 Washington is said to have warned it would halt such talks unless the legal protections of Coupang chairman Kim Bom - an American citizen also known as Kim Bom-suk - were guaranteed The Coupang fine will "again draw backlash from the US" for its size, said Kim Dae-jong, professor of business at Sejong University in Seoul. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

matched_articles.title: S. Korea fines e-commerce giant Coupang record $525 million over data leak
matched_articles.summary: Coupang 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'S. Korea fines e-commerce giant Coupang record $525 million over data leak'이다. The move caps a months-long probe into the country’s largest online retail platform. PHOTO: AFP Published Jun 11, 2026, 10:48 AM Updated Jun 11, 2026, 05:43 PM SEOUL – South Korea imposed on June 11 a record US$408 million (S$525 million) fine on e-commerce giant Coupang over a leak that exposed the data of more than 30 million customers, provoking ire from US lawmakers on Coupang for violating safety obligations and collecting personal data without legal grounds,” a Personal Information Protection Commission statement said 5 million users being exposed, the commission said Its probe also found that “Coupang had unlawfully collected the online activity records of about 11 The US is said to have warned it could halt such discussions unless the legal protections of Coupang chairman Kim Bom – an American citizen also known as Kim Bom-suk – were guaranteed. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

(Photo by Yuki Nakao) KIM JAEWON June 11, 2026 05:01 JST TOKYO -- South Korea's SK Hynix will triple its wafer capacity by 2034 to meet soaring demand for its memory chips critical for artificial intelligence computing, SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won said in an exclusive interview with Nikkei Asia on Wednesday. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

matched_articles.title: Hyundai expects Chennai Plant 1 production to normalise by June 15 after Mobis fire
matched_articles.summary: Hyundai Motor 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Hyundai expects Chennai Plant 1 production to normalise by June 15 after Mobis fire'이다. The automaker expects all operations to return to normal by June 22; Says retail sales in June 2026 are unlikely to be significantly affected. com/companies/hyundai-expects-chennai-plant-1-production-to-normalise-by-june-15-after-mobis-fire/article71083471 Sources said the disruption was largely limited to Chennai Plant 1, where Hyundai manufactures SUVs (file photo) | Photo Credit: ANI Production at Hyundai Motor India’s Chennai Plant 1 in Sriperumbudur, which was affected by a fire at key supplier Mobis India Ltd on May 31, is expected to regain pace by June 15, with all operations returning to normal by June 22, the company said in a stock exchange filing on Wednesday The disruption was largely limited to Chennai Plant 1, where Hyundai manufactures sport utility vehicles (SUVs), said sources Hyundai said it has initiated multiple measures to minimise the impact of the disruption, including sourcing automotive components from alternate locations to restore production levels Sources said Mobis supplies audio systems and certain other components used in Hyundai vehicles. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.
